signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Tension + Carry + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.005 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage sustains the bid despite US-Iran talks hitting a snag. JPY COT LF net short -61,340 (crowded carry) remains vulnerable to intervention headlines, yet the USD/JPY vs US 10Y correlation (z=-2.05σ) confirms a breakdown in traditional yield sensitivity as geopolitical risk-premia and funding stress dominate. Carry remains the primary driver while VIX remains at 17.9 (normal volatility).

signals.title: AUD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/JPY at 113.961 — G8 bilateral theme active as AUD outperformance (+0.523%) meets JPY weakness (-0.504%). RBA 4.35% vs BoJ 0.75% provides a +360bp carry premium, supporting the hawkish RBA outlook. Retail sentiment is 85% short, diverging from COT LF net long +58,994; this institutional vs retail divergence suggests a squeeze higher if intervention risks at 114.00+ do not materialize.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5970 — COT LF net short -19,294 (specs fading the bird) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for the next meeting. If RBNZ delivers a hawkish hold or signals a hike, short-squeeze risk is elevated as specs are positioned for a cut (long% at 18.5%). Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25% creates a +150bp USD carry advantage, but the positioning extreme suggests the downside is exhausted.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Election Shock + Positioning
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3651 — Pair remains resilient despite the UK election shock, supported by a neutral 0bp carry spread vs the Fed. GBP COT LF net long +31,244 (65.3% long) indicates institutional support for the currency despite political uncertainty. Resistance holds near 1.3700 as markets digest the 'higher-for-longer' Fed outlook and US-Iran headlines.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Retail Sentiment Extreme
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7772 — Retail sentiment is 85% long, creating a contrarian reversal risk as the pair tests recent lows. Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0.0% provides a massive +375bp carry advantage for USD, yet price action remains heavy (1M: -1.79%). Retail is aggressively fading the CHF strength; a break of 0.7750 likely triggers retail capitulation.
